sections.paragraph:
not used in Kal. Arabic ساحت to destroy.

Piel שִׁחֵת-

(1) trans., to destroy, to ruin, either by laying waste, as a country, a region, a field, Gen 9:11 19:13 Jos 22:33 2Sa 24:16 Jer 12:10, or by pulling down, as walls, a city, Gen 13:10 Eze 26:4 or by wounding, Exo 21:26 or by killing, both individuals, 2Sa 1:14, and whole peoples, Gen 6:17 9:15 Num 32:15 Isa 14:20. Metaph., Amo 1:11, שִׁחַת רַחֲמָיו “he destroyed (i.e. suppressed) his mercy.” Eze 28:17, שִׁחַתָּ חָכְמָֽתְךָ עַל־יִפְעָתֶךָ “thou hast destroyed thy wisdom, because of thy beauty;” thou art so taken with the latter, that thou hast neglected the former.

(2) to act wickedly, for the more full שִׁחֵת דְּרָכָיו Exo 32:7 Deu 9:12 32:5 . Comp. Hiph. No. 2.

Hiphil-

(1) i.q. Piel No. 1, to destroy, either by laying waste and destroying, as a land, a kingdom, Jer 36:29 51:20 a city, Gen 19:14 2Ki 18:25 or by killing, as a man, 1Sa 26:15 enemies, 2Sa 11:1 or by harming in any manner whatever, Pro 11:9. הַמַּלְאָךְ הַמַּשְׁחִית the destroying angel, 2Sa 24:16 and simply הַמַּשְׁחִית Exo 12:23, is the angel of God who inflicts calamities and death upon men. אַל תַּשְׁחֵת (destroy not) in the titles of Psa 57:1-11, 58 , 59 , 75 , seems to be the beginning of a song, to the tune of which those Psalms were to be sung.

(2) הִשְׁחִית דַּרְכֹּו Gen 6:12 הִ׳ עֲלִילֹות Zep 3:7 to act wickedly, and without the acc. id., Deu 4:16 31:29 Jud. 2:19 Isa 1:4. Compare הֵרֵעַ. In the words אִישׁ מַשְׁחִית Pro 28:24, and בַּעַל מַשְׁחִית Pro 18:9, מ׳ appears to be a subst. (see מַשְׁחִית No. 1 ), a man of destruction, i.e. in chap. 28 , act., a destroyer, a waster; but in chap. 18 , pass., one who brings destruction on himself, one who wastes his own goods, a prodigal.

Hophal הָשְׁחַת to be corrupted, Pro 25:26 Mal 1:14.

Niphal-

(1) to be corrupted, e.g. by putridity, Jer 13:7 in a moral sense, Gen 6:11, 12 .

(2) to be laid waste, Exo 8:20.

Derivatives, מַשְׁחִית, מִשְׁחָת, מַשְׁחֵת, מָשְׁחָת.
